candidate_sources.title: Alder - Sooty Mold - Cooperative Extension: Insect Pests, Ticks and Plant Diseases - University of Maine Cooperative Extension
candidate_sources.snippet: The pathogen causing sooty mold on alder is Scorias spongiosa, and it is associated with aphid and scale pests of alder, pine, beech and other trees. Sooty mold is a charcoal black fungus that appears on the upper surface of leaves, twigs, branches and fruits.

candidate_sources.title: Sooty Molds | NC State Extension Publications
candidate_sources.snippet: Sooty molds are not real pathogens; they colonize the honeydew excreted from aphids and other phloem-feeding, sap-sucking insects and feed on the sugary substance. Sooty molds lessen the aesthetic value of ornamental plants, and lower the vigor of plants by blocking sunlight essential for photosynthesis.

candidate_sources.title: Sooty Mold : Landscape : Center for Agriculture, Food, and the Environment (CAFE) at UMass Amherst
candidate_sources.snippet: Capnodium is one of the common genera of sooty mold fungi. Sooty mold is a dark colored fungus that grows on honeydew excreted by piercing sucking insects such as mealybugs, leafhoppers, scales and aphids.

candidate_sources.title: Woolly Alder Aphids - BYGL (osu.edu)
candidate_sources.snippet: Woolly Alder Aphids (Prociphilus tessellatus) produce large, white fluffy colonies on the branches of their namesake host (Alnus spp.). Woolly alder and beech blight aphids both produce copious quantities of honeydew which becomes colonized by a specific sooty mold fungus, Scorias spongiosa.
